LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS JOHN ORF, AND, KYLE ORF MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS BEEN DILATORY AND THE PROPOSED AMENDMENT PLEADS A NEW THEORY OF LIABILITY AGAINST K&S ON THE EVE OF TRIAL. PLAINTIFF WAS AWARE OF THE FACTS SUPPORTING THE AMENDMENT NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 2009. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: JOYCE LONG, CSR# 8620

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Feb-01-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge Ellen Chaitin., Clerk: J. Willis-McGhee, CSR: Karen Held, #2857

Alison Kwan, Esq. appeared for plaintiff John Orf, who was not present at today's hearing.
No appearance by defendant Able Credit Services.
Default was entered against defendant on Oct. 13, 2011.
Dismissal with prejudice as to defendant the K & S was filed on Aug. 6, 2010.
Doe defendants were dismissed on Oct. 13, 2010.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff John Orf, and against the Defaulted Defendant Able Credit Services as follows:

Damages/Principal - $103,900.00; Attorney's Fees - $112,154.94, for a total judgment in the amount of $216,054.94

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Inspection Demands; Production Of Documents; And Verifications From Pltfs To Inspection Demands, Set Two And Special Interrogatories, Set Two; Appendix
Off Calendar May-21-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT K & S CO., INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Inspection Demands, Production Of Documents, And Verifications From Plaintiffs To Inspection Demands, Set Two, And Special Interrogatories, Set Two, is granted in part and denied in part.
As to the Document Requests to Kyle Orf, the motion is granted as to Nos. 7-14 as the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. The responses otherwise are adequate.
As to the Document Requests to John Orf. The motion is granted as to Nos. 6-9 as the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. The responses otherwise are adequate.
The motion is denied as to Nos. 10-12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 20, and 22.
The motion is granted as to No. 21 as the request does not seek attorney client communications.
The court did not have any briefing on Special Interrogatories.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
All requests for sanctions are denied. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

Off Calendar May-14-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS JOHN ORF, and, KYLE ORF Motion for Leave to File First Amended Complaint. Appearances: Drexel A. Bradshaw, Esq. Bradshaw & Associates (415) 433-4800, appearing for Plaintiffs and moving party; Adam M. Koss, Esq. of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney (415) 788-1900, appearing for Defendant and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. Plaintiff has been dilatory and the proposed amendment pleads a new theory of liability against K&S on the eve of trial. Plaintiff was aware of the facts supporting the amendment no later than November 2009. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Joyce Long, CSR# 8620
